^Noah: We're not after you. Based on your past environments, you might have some important information and we figured that sharing it in front of your people might be difficult for you.
^The woman returns with an aspirin and a glass of water.
^Troy: Honey, I offered to show the detectives around the neighborhood, I think I might be able to help them.
^Young Woman: Oh, alright. Please keep us posted if anything comes up. It's a safe neighborhood and we need to keep it that way.

c-2: ^Noah: What do you know exactly about this group of people ?
^Troy: They call it "Arbor". It's latin, I think it means "tree", or something. But don't think of it as a glorified crew. We're talking about an entire secret society.

^Troy: They have a strict hierarchical system, their own moral code and a specific agenda. I guess they resemble the Mob in that way. They also bear a symbol on their skin, it basically looks like the letter "A", but it's not easy to see. They bear it in some part that is typically covered by clothing.

c-0: ^Troy: I don't know how to put this. I understand that they recruit young people systematically. From this point forward, these people become their "soldiers". They're given new identities, while being force-fed the "Arbor" propaganda.
^Troy: They believe that our civilization is bound for a revolution. Just like it happens every few centuries, when the status quo becomes obsolete in the face of societal challenges.
^Troy: Obviously, everything was in shambles, after the war. Hence, the establishment of the New Republic, a democratic regime designed to regain the population's trust.
^Troy: Well, for a lot of people, not only did the New Republic fail to restore the overall living standard, but the flimsy structure of this regime enabled lawlessness, corruption and chaos.
^Troy: That's where "Arbor" come into play. Their mission statement orders for an underground manipulation of key political and social affairs, which in time will enable them to stage a coup.
^Noah: Are you off your meds ? Do you hear what comes out of your mouth ?
^Troy: You think I sound like a nutcase, right ? I get it, but it goes to show how well they're covering their tracks. Trust me, they have people in your line of work, too.
c-1: ^Noah: Are we dealing with fascists here ?
^Troy: It's a bit more complicated. They don't discriminate racially while recruiting. That being said, they believe that humanity shall be governed by the "Archetype".
^Troy: That is a person who not only has reached their upper physical, mental and intellectual limits, but can also be ruthless in the name of order.
c-2: ^Noah: I can't say I blame them. For the past 30 years, all our hopes for a better future have been flushed down the drain by these so-called moralists.
^Troy: For a while, I was conditioned to think that way, too. However, I've seen the things they're willing to do and I snapped out of it.

b: ^If true, these are very serious allegations
^Umm, could you slowly repeat, like, everything ??

c-1: ^Noah: What was your involvement in all of this ? How did you contribute ?

^Troy: I never had an active role within their main operations, just to make things clear. I would strictly serve them through public relations. They found my networking to be very useful and influential.

^Troy: It basically means that I would try to introduce important people of various industries to the "Arbor" people. I would find opportunities at corporate events, business meetings, concerts, art exhibitions, and so forth.
^Troy: Obviously, I never exposed "Arbor". I would simply entice outsiders to get in contact with the right people by false pretences. Simple networking.
^"What sort of people were you fishing for?"

c-0: ^Noah: Looks to me like you were heavily involved, then.

^Troy: I'm no saint, I'll admit it. But that's as far as I ever went with them. They make their connections either way, I was just another drone. Still, I never forced anything. Whoever ended up doing business with them, did so willingly.
c-1: ^Noah: What sort of people were you fishing for ?

^Troy: Highly influential people from my circles. Could be working in tech, pharmaceutics, stocks, politics, you name it, even in showbiz. Specifically, people who I knew would always crave more power, no matter what.
g-0: ^Troy: Make no mistake though, I stopped enjoying it very quickly. The more time went on, the more I wanted to distance myself. But they were being manipulative, trying to make me feel ostracized if I didn't do my part.

c-2: ^Troy: I started fearing for my life ! Once I had become considerably detached, I started receiving subtle threats.
^Troy: Well, that and... a little friction from some business deals... They thought that I was trying to take advantage of them.

talkaboutex: ^Noah: I would like to discuss your former female partner. As per protocol, I need to write down some basic information, as provided by you. Full name, ethnicity, age, for a start.

b: ^Troy: I'm not sure I follow you. This must be a mistake, I had no "partner" during any of this.

c-0: ^Noah: I assume you're afraid that this information will reach the wrong ears. Believe me, the only people in my precinct who aknowledge your existence are me and my partner.

b: ^regarded you as a potential threat to our nefarious interests, we would have taken other measures

c-1: ^Noah: I'm afraid you don't have much of a choice. Your other option would have you being charged with conspiracy under the state of federal law and human trafficking.

^Noah: For us two, we put another one behind bars, earn our paycheck, ascend the hierarchy. These cases are often dragged out too much, at some point we're looking for a scapegoat to take the fall.
^Noah: When it comes to you, though, how would it serve you to have yourself exposed to every headline and news broadcast in the state ? Wouldn't that draw your former associates' attention ?
^Noah: Do you think they would be relieved that you took one for the team or would they be on their toes thinking you could rat on them at any minute to cut yourself a deal ?
^Noah: Would you like constantly living in fear of your own shadow ?

c-1: ^Troy: God... Why not hold a knife to my neck instead... Alright.

talkaboutex_proceed: ^Troy: Her name is Cristina Artuso. Long-haired brunette, early thirties, of Italian descent.
^Troy: She used to live in that neo-classical, upper-class residence, Stilton Avenue, 48. I don't know if she still lives there, haven't spoken to her in years, obviously.

^Troy: I met her about a decade ago. She came to an art exhibition I was hosting. I sold her what she thought was an authentic unknown painting of Caravaggio, so we started talking. This particular style really intrigued her, probably reminded her of her anscestry.

^Troy: Anyway, shortly after, we started dating. At first, I thought everything was perfect, we lived separately and nothing seemed off. She'd only get a little tense whenever we would go on trips, but I just figured she couldn't be away from work.
^Troy: About a year later, we'd gotten more serious, so I asked her to move in with me, but she turned me down. I didn't get it then.

^Troy: As I would spend more and more time at her place, I also got to know the other residents of the building. I discovered that she would spend a lot of time with them and eventually, so did I, by association.
mentionskid: ^Noah: I'm here because a mutual 'friend' of ours, 'Skid', told me you could help us with something.
^Troy: I'm sorry, I have no clue who you're referring to.

b: ^ He avoids eye contact and his inflection sounds unnatural... He's clearly lying.

c-0: ^Noah: Lying won't do you any favors, you've given yourself away. 'Skid' told me all about your past as a con-artist.

b: ^Troy: I guess I should thank you for not confronting me in front of my wife. She knows very little about my past life and I need to ensure that, for everyone's sake.

troys_gift: ^ are curiously awaiting as Troy goes through his impressive gadget kit. The man presents to them some sort of a small computer.

^Troy: It basically allows you to read and copy all hidden files stored in any type of drive, no matter the encryption level. Once you connect it, it can also break through any security wall. I dedicated years of my life and quite a bit of my sanity to develop this, but it's truly remarkable.
^Troy: However, it requires some basic knowledge of operating systems in order to be able to use it properly.
^Troy: Once I realized that my days in Cristina's world, or the world in general might be numbered, I secretly started to retrieve files from her devices.

^Troy: I couldn't go through her devices that often, because she was very wary about them. However, a couple of times, she accidentally let her "work" phone exposed and I managed to extract some data.
^Troy: From what I understand, she also had other much more private streams of communication, whatever that could be. Still, you can take this for what it's worth.
^Troy hands the computer over to Noah.
